sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund the Cardano Builder DAO as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for builder-led treasury allocation tied to Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with treasury withdrawal administered by an independent council/multisig composed of dRep DAO members before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL., Operate proposal evaluation, feedback coordination, transparent fund distribution, KPI reporting, and increasingly on-chain KPI verification., Continue and improve DAO KPI dashboards and work with dReps to standardize KPI tracking., Disburse funds only after milestone completion is validated and confirmed by the administrator., Return unused or unallocated DAO treasury funds to the Cardano Treasury.

Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is designed to fund builders whose work can improve Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs including monthly active users, monthly transactions, and TVL.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Funded projects are required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions through public reporting and increasingly on-chain KPI verification.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Round 1 involved 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, funded 20 companies, had 83% governance participation, and distributed 5,541,335 ADA through a smart contract-governed process.
Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): Round 2 added 18 new members, funded 14 companies after review and voting, and had 88% member participation.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The DAO created and amended governing documents, elected a board, added third-party assurance, implemented merit-based proposal review, and executed treasury withdrawals on-chain.
missing: Independent evidence for: Funded projects are required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions through public reporting and increasingly on-chain KPI verification.
Independent evidence for: The DAO has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Independent evidence for: Round 1 involved 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, funded 20 companies, had 83% governance participation, and distributed 5,541,335 ADA through a smart contract-governed process.
Independent evidence for: Round 2 added 18 new members, funded 14 companies after review and voting, and had 88% member participation.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is designed to fund builders whose work can improve Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs including monthly active users, monthly transactions, and TVL." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Funded projects are required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions through public reporting and increasingly on-chain KPI verification." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
